data_health_narrative: Overall health is reduced by high-severity contract violations. Fix CRITICAL fields to prevent silent corruption downstream.

violations_this_week.description: Week 2 Digital Courtroom: verdict records failed structured LLM output schema validation. Downstream AI consumers can ingest malformed scores or evidence and produce incorrect risk decisions.

Week 3 Document Refinery: `extracted_facts[*].confidence` must stay a float in 0.0–1.0. Downstream systems that interpret confidence as a probability (Week 4 lineage attribution and any scoring consumers) may mis-rank or mis-blame changes.

Week 3 Document Refinery: `extracted_facts[*].entity_refs` must reference IDs present in `entities[]`. Downstream lineage metadata can become inconsistent, breaking entity-aware attribution and any audit joins.

schema_changes_detected: Week 3 extraction records: Field `extracted_facts` changed type from `array` to `object`. Breaking change requires coordinated consumer updates.
Week 5 event records: Field `payload` changed type from `object` to `integer`. Breaking change requires coordinated consumer updates.
ai_system_risk_assessment: Embedding drift is within acceptable bounds.
LLM output schema violation rate is stable.

recommended_actions.action: Update `src/week3/extractor.py` so `extracted_facts[*].confidence` is emitted as a float in the 0.0–1.0 range (contract clause: `week3.extracted_facts.confidence.range`).

Update `src/week3/extractor.py` so every `extracted_facts[*].entity_refs[]` value is an `entity_id` present in the same record’s `entities[]` array (contract clause: `week3.extracted_facts.entity_refs.relationship`).
Re-run AI extensions after any model/prompt change and monitor `validation_reports/ai_metrics.json` for rising schema violation rate.
